pattern: Fix the target score as the sum of the first two elements, then greedily count how many consecutive pairs from the front each sum to that target.
hints: The score is locked to nums[0]+nums[1] after the very first operation; all subsequent operations must match this sum.
Scan the array two elements at a time from the front; as long as the pair sum matches the target, increment the operation count.
Stop as soon as a pair sum differs from the target or fewer than two elements remain.

edge_inputs.description: second example, only two pairs match
all pairs match the first sum
only one valid operation because second pair differs

stress.description: random arrays to exercise early termination
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
